div.head: 280. Backchannel Message From the President’s Deputy Assistant for National Security Affairs (Haig) to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Kissinger) in New DelhiSource: National Archives, Nixon Presidential Materials, NSC Files, Box 432, Backchannel Files, Very Sensitive Trip Cables. Top Secret; Sensitive; Exclusively Eyes Only. On Haig’s behalf, Kennedy initialed the message, which was sent from the White House at 1:04 p.m.
div.p: WH 10611/Sitto 42. To: David Halperin for Henry A. Kissinger.

The President has called me in for a second time just prior to our departure for San Clemente.See Document 279. He is increasingly concerned about response from our messengerDobrynin. See Document 273. and its future implications. He asked me to re-emphasize to you his desire that intermediate visitSee footnote 6, Document 278. be cut out and proceed directly to the main round. Concerning your other discussions in the Kirschman forum,Reference is to Kissinger’s secret talks with the North Vietnamese in Paris. Walters, who was responsible for managing the channel, gave Kissinger the pseudonym “Harold A. Kirschman.” (Walters, Secret Missions, p. 514) See also Kissinger, White House Years, p. 439. he feels strongly that they be terminated and that the record is made clearly by Kirschman.

Considerable concern has been expressed about the Danang shellingEarly in the morning on July 5, North Vietnamese artillery struck the U.S. airbase at Danang, killing three American soldiers. and he wants it clearly conveyed that future activity of this kind will not be tolerated nor will tit-for-tat modus operandi be pursued.
